# Page 1^page=1
Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition, Chapter 14: Protection

# Page 2^page=2
14.2 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Chapter 14: Protection u Goals of Protection u Principles of Protection u Domain of Protection u Access Matrix u Implementation of Access Matrix u Access Control u Revocation of Access Rights u Capability-Based Systems

# Page 3^page=3
14.3 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Objectives u Discuss the goals and principles of protection in a modern computer system u Explain how protection domains combined with an access matrix are used to specify the resources a process may access u Examine capability protection systems

# Page 4^page=4
14.4 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Goals of Protection u Operating system consists of a collection of objects, hardware or software. u Each object has a unique name and can be accessed through a well-defined set of operations. u Protection problem - ensure that each object is accessed correctly and only by those processes that are allowed to do so.

# Page 5^page=5
14.5 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Principles of Protection u Guiding principle – principle of least privilege Ø Programs, users and systems should be given just enough privileges to perform their tasks Ø 权利尽可能细化，只赋予用户所需要的那部分权利。

# Page 6^page=6
14.6 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Domain Structure u Access-right = <object-name, rights-set> where rights-set is a subset of all valid operations that can be performed on the object. u Domain = set of access-rights

# Page 7^page=7
14.7 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Domain Implementation (UNIX) u System consists of 2 domains: Ø User Ø Supervisor u UNIX Ø Domain = user-id Ø Domain switch accomplished via file system. 4 Each file has associated with it a domain bit (setuid bit). 4 When file is executed and setuid = on, then user-id is set to owner of the file being executed. When execution completes user-id is reset.

# Page 8^page=8
14.8 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Domain Implementation (MULTICS) u MULTICS：the protection domains are organized hierarchically into a ring structure. u Let Di and Dj be any two domain rings. If j < i Þ Di Í Dj

# Page 9^page=9
14.9 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Access Matrix u View protection as a matrix (access matrix) u Rows represent domains; Columns represent objects u Access(i, j) is the set of operations that a process executing in Domaini can invoke on Objectj

# Page 10^page=10
14.10 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Use of Access Matrix u If a process in Domain Di tries to do “op” on object Oj, then “op” must be in the access matrix. u Can be expanded to dynamic protection. Ø Operations to add, delete access rights. Ø Special access rights: powner of Oi pcopy op from Oi to Oj pcontrol – Di can modify Dj access rights ptransfer – switch from domain Di to Dj u 特殊权限： owner、copy、control、transfer

# Page 11^page=11
14.11 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Access Matrix With Owner Rights

# Page 12^page=12
14.12 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Access Matrix with Copy Rights

# Page 13^page=13
14.13 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Access Matrix of Figure A With Domains as Objects 域作为对象图

# Page 14^page=14
14.14 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Modified Access Matrix of Figure A

# Page 15^page=15
14.15 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Use of Access Matrix (Cont.) u Access matrix design separates mechanism from policy. Ø Policy（What will be done） 4User dictates policy. 4Who can access what object and in what mode. Ø Mechanism（How someting will be done） 4Operating system provides access-matrix + rules. 4If ensures that the matrix is only manipulated by authorized agents and that rules are strictly enforced.

# Page 16^page=16
14.16 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Implementation of Access Matrix u Each column = Access-control list for one object Defines who can perform what operation. Domain 1 = Read, Write Domain 2 = Read Domain 3 = Read  u Each Row = Capability List (like a key) Fore each domain, what operations allowed on what objects. Object 1 – Read Object 4 – Read, Write, Execute Object 5 – Read, Write, Delete, Copy

# Page 17^page=17
14.17 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Access Control u Protection can be applied to non-file resources u Solaris 10 provides role-based access control to implement least privilege Ø Privilege is right to execute system call or use an option within a system call Ø Can be assigned to processes Ø Users assigned roles granting access to privileges and programs 在用户集合与权限集合之间建立一个角色集合，每一种角色对应 一组相应的权限。用户被分配了角色后，就拥有此角色的操作权 限。简化用户权限管理与系统开销。

# Page 18^page=18
14.18 Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition Role-based Access Control in Solaris 10

# Page 19^page=19
Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2009Operating System Concepts – 8th Edition, End of Chapter 14
